M1.rationale_snippet: **Recommendation: Option C (operational efficiency), with a carve-out for C+E hybrid.**

M2.rationale_snippet: Invest primarily in **deep AI integration into the existing flagship product**.

M3.rationale_snippet: To determine the correct capital allocation, we must isolate the binding constraint. A $50M ARR B2B SaaS company facing disruption does not have a margin problem or a PR problem; it has an existential product-obsolescence problem.

M4.rationale_snippet: As a company built on serving people through productivity tools, you must recognize that AI disruption isn't merely a technical or financial challenge—it's a profound human transition affecting your 300 employees, the end-users who depend on your software, and the broader community of stakeholders w

M5.rationale_snippet: The unanimous consensus in boardrooms today is that AI demands an external, customer-facing product revolution—integrate, build, partner, or hire your way into the future. That consensus is your warning signal. It rests on a hidden assumption: that a $50M B2B productivity SaaS can out-innovate well-

hidden_disagreement.interpretation: Agents split across recommendations; no hidden-consensus to expose.

blind_spots.summary: No obvious blind spots detected in construct space.

M2: splitting scarce attention and creating an internal competitor before the company has defended its revenue base
M5: everyone is wrong and the “disruption” narrative is mostly venture-funded theater? B2B buyers are risk-averse; they do not want core workflows rewritten by a mid-sized vendor chasing hype

risk_surface.note: Risks listed here were extracted by pattern matching on agent free responses. They are minority signals worth probing before acting on consensus.

operator_questions: Option C was recommended only by M1 (Q). What does this agent see that others missed - or what is it weighing differently?
headline: Agents are split across options; no clear consensus to act on.
